input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a value for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI listens to the OS theme change event automatically — no extra wiring needed.

---

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme<T>()`

The equivalent in code-behind or any C# context uses the `SetAppTheme` extension method on any `BindableObject`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
this.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,          // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

myLabel.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

myButton.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

Under the hood this creates the same `AppThemeBinding`; it reacts to runtime theme changes without a restart.

---

### Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Override** | Set `Application.UserAppTheme` to force a theme app-wide |
| **Resources** | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside a `ResourceDictionary` style so every control picks it up |
| **Unspecified** | Falls back to the `Light` value when the OS doesn't report a preference |

This approach keeps all theme logic declarative and lets the OS drive the switch.
